description: Single Page Application built with React

llm_prompt.title: Integrate with a Coding Agent Prompt
llm_prompt.description: Use AI to generate integration code for your React application
llm_prompt.content:
# Integrate {{productName}} Authentication in React Application (Inbuilt Mode)

## Context
I have a React application and I want to integrate {{productName}}'s authentication system using the ThunderID React SDK with {{productName}}-hosted login pages.

## Requirements
- Use @thunderid/react SDK for authentication
- Configure {{productName}}-hosted login, registration, and account management UIs
- Implement sign-in and sign-out functionality using prebuilt components
- Display signed-in user's profile information
- Handle authentication state automatically

## Configuration
- **Client ID**: {{clientId}}
- **Base URL**: https://localhost:8090 (or your {{productName}} instance URL)
- **SDK**: @thunderid/react

## IMPORTANT Configuration Rules
- DO NOT create a separate config object - pass all configuration as individual props directly to <ThunderIDProvider>
- Required props: `clientId` and `baseUrl`
- Optional props: `afterSignInUrl`, `afterSignOutUrl`, `scopes` (string array)
- NEVER use property names like `signInRedirectURL` or `signOutRedirectURL`
- Example: <ThunderIDProvider clientId="xxx" baseUrl="xxx">

## Implementation Steps
1. Create a React app using Vite by running: `npm create vite@latest react-demo -- --template react --rolldown --no-interactive`
2. Navigate into the project directory: `cd react-demo`
3. Install dependencies: `npm install`
4. Install @thunderid/react package: `npm install @thunderid/react`
5. Wrap your app with <ThunderIDProvider /> and configure clientId and baseUrl
6. Add <SignInButton /> for signed-out users and <SignOutButton /> for signed-in users
7. Use <SignedIn> and <SignedOut> components to conditionally render UI
8. Display user profile with <UserDropdown /> component
9. Run the development server: `npm run dev`

Please provide complete, working code with proper configuration for {{productName}} authentication using the ThunderID React SDK.

manual_steps.title: Create a React app using Vite
manual_steps.description: Run the following command to create a new React app with Vite:

code.content:
npm create vite@latest react-demo -- --template react --rolldown --no-interactive
cd react-demo
npm install

manual_steps.title: Install @thunderid/react
manual_steps.description: The ThunderID React SDK provides prebuilt components, hooks, and helpers for {{productName}} authentication.
manual_steps.subDescription: Run the following command to install the SDK:

manual_steps.title: Add <ThunderIDProvider /> to your app
manual_steps.description: In your main.jsx or index.jsx, wrap your application with the ThunderIDProvider component:

code.content:
import { StrictMode } from 'react'
import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'
import './index.css'
import App from './App.jsx'
import { ThunderIDProvider } from '@thunderid/react'

createRoot(document.getElementById('root')).render(
  <StrictMode>
    <ThunderIDProvider
      clientId="{{clientId}}"
      baseUrl="https://localhost:8090"
    >
      <App />
    </ThunderIDProvider>
  </StrictMode>
)

manual_steps.title: Add <SignInButton /> and <SignOutButton /> to your app
manual_steps.description: Replace the existing content of the App.jsx file with following content:

code.content:
import { SignedIn, SignedOut, SignInButton, SignOutButton } from '@thunderid/react'
import './App.css'

function App() {
  return (
    <header>
      <SignedIn>
        <SignOutButton />
      </SignedIn>
      <SignedOut>
        <SignInButton />
      </SignedOut>
    </header>
  )
}

export default App

manual_steps.title: Display signed-in user's profile information
manual_steps.description: Add the UserDropdown component to display user profile information:

code.content:
import { SignedIn, SignedOut, SignInButton, SignOutButton, UserDropdown } from '@thunderid/react'
import './App.css'

function App() {
  return (
    <>
      <header>
        <SignedIn>
          <UserDropdown />
          <SignOutButton />
        </SignedIn>
        <SignedOut>
          <SignInButton />
        </SignedOut>
      </header>
    </>
  )
}

export default App

manual_steps.title: Run the app
manual_steps.description: Start your development server and test the authentication flow:

llm_prompt.title: Integrate with a Coding Agent Prompt
llm_prompt.description: Use AI to generate integration code for your React application
llm_prompt.content:
# Integrate {{productName}} Authentication in React Application (Custom Mode) ## Context I have a React application and I want to integrate {{productName}}'s authentication system using the ThunderID React SDK with a custom login UI instead of {{productName}}-hosted pages. ## Requirements - Use @thunderid/react SDK for authentication - Build custom login UI with the component - Use react-router-dom for routing to the custom sign-in page - Configure custom signInUrl in ThunderIDProvider - Implement sign-in and sign-out functionality - Display signed-in user's profile information - Handle authentication state automatically ## Configuration - **Application ID**: {{applicationId}} - **Base URL**: https://localhost:8090 (or your {{productName}} instance URL) - **Sign In URL**: http://localhost:5173/signin (custom route) - **SDK**: @thunderid/react - **Router**: react-router-dom ## IMPORTANT Configuration Rules - DO NOT create a separate config object - pass all configuration as individual props directly to - Required props: `baseUrl`, `signInUrl`, and `applicationId` - Optional props: `afterSignInUrl`, `afterSignOutUrl`, `scopes` - NEVER use property names like `signInRedirectURL` or `signOutRedirectURL` - Example: ## Available SDK APIs ### Hook: useThunderID (ONLY hook available) - The SDK provides ONLY ONE hook: `useThunderID` - No other hooks like useAuth, useSession, useUser, etc. exist - Use this hook to access authentication state and methods Example: ```javascript import { useThunderID } from '@thunderid/react'; const MyComponent = () => { const { isSignedIn, user, signIn, signOut } = useThunderID(); return (
{isSignedIn ? ( <>

Welcome, {user.displayName}!

) : ( )}
); }; ``` ### User Data Components (use if needed) 1. **User Component** - Render props for user data: ```javascript import { User } from '@thunderid/react' Please sign in

}> {(user) => (

Welcome, {user?.displayName}!

Email: {user?.email}

)} ``` 2. **UserProfile Component** - Pre-built user profile UI: ```javascript import { UserProfile } from '@thunderid/react' ``` ## Implementation Steps 1. Create a React app using Vite by running: `npm create vite@latest react-demo -- --template react --rolldown --no-interactive` 2. Navigate into the project directory: `cd react-demo` 3. Install dependencies: `npm install` 4. Install react-router-dom package: `npm install react-router-dom` 5. Install @thunderid/react package: `npm install @thunderid/react` 6. Wrap your app with and configure baseUrl, signInUrl, and applicationId 7. Set up React Router with BrowserRouter 8. Add for signed-out users and for signed-in users 9. Create a /signin route and integrate the component 10. Use and components to conditionally render UI 11. Display user profile with component or use useThunderID hook if needed 12. Run the development server: `npm run dev` Please provide complete, working code with: - Proper routing configuration - Custom sign-in page integration - Proper integration with the ThunderID React SDK - Use of useThunderID hook if programmatic access to auth state is needed

manual_steps.title: Create a React app using Vite
manual_steps.description: Run the following command to create a new React app with Vite:

code.content:
npm create vite@latest react-demo -- --template react --rolldown --no-interactive
cd react-demo
npm install

manual_steps.title: Install react-router
manual_steps.description: Install react-router-dom for routing to the custom sign-in page:

manual_steps.title: Install @thunderid/react
manual_steps.description: The ThunderID React SDK provides prebuilt components, hooks, and helpers for {{productName}} authentication:

manual_steps.title: Add <ThunderIDProvider /> to your app
manual_steps.description: In your main.jsx or index.jsx, wrap your application with the ThunderIDProvider component and configure the signInUrl:

code.content:
import { StrictMode } from 'react'
import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'
import './index.css'
import App from './App.jsx'
import { ThunderIDProvider } from '@thunderid/react'

createRoot(document.getElementById('root')).render(
  <StrictMode>
    <ThunderIDProvider
      baseUrl="https://localhost:8090"
      signInUrl="http://localhost:5173/signin"
      applicationId="{{applicationId}}"
    >
      <App />
    </ThunderIDProvider>
  </StrictMode>
)

manual_steps.title: Add <SignInButton /> and <SignOutButton /> to your app
manual_steps.description: Replace the existing content of the App.jsx file with following content:

code.content:
import { SignedIn, SignedOut, SignInButton, SignOutButton } from '@thunderid/react'
import './App.css'

function App() {
  return (
    <header>
      <SignedIn>
        <SignOutButton />
      </SignedIn>
      <SignedOut>
        <SignInButton />
      </SignedOut>
    </header>
  )
}

export default App

manual_steps.title: Integrate the <SignIn /> component
manual_steps.description: Create a new route /signin and add the <SignIn /> component to handle the sign-in process:

code.content:
import { BrowserRouter as Router, Routes, Route } from 'react-router-dom'
import { SignIn, SignedIn, SignedOut, SignInButton, SignOutButton } from '@thunderid/react'
import './App.css'

function App() {
  return (
    <Router>
      <header>
        <SignedIn>
          <SignOutButton />
        </SignedIn>
        <SignedOut>
          <SignInButton />
        </SignedOut>
      </header>
      <Routes>
        <Route path="/signin" element={<SignIn />} />
      </Routes>
    </Router>
  )
}

export default App

manual_steps.title: Display signed-in user's profile information
manual_steps.description: Add the UserDropdown component to display user profile information:

code.content:
import { BrowserRouter as Router, Routes, Route } from 'react-router-dom'
import { SignIn, SignedIn, SignedOut, SignInButton, SignOutButton, UserDropdown } from '@thunderid/react'
import './App.css'

function App() {
  return (
    <Router>
      <header>
        <SignedIn>
          <UserDropdown />
          <SignOutButton />
        </SignedIn>
        <SignedOut>
          <SignInButton />
        </SignedOut>
      </header>
      <Routes>
        <Route path="/signin" element={<SignIn />} />
      </Routes>
    </Router>
  )
}

export default App

manual_steps.description: Start your development server and test the authentication flow with custom sign-in page:
